calendar.matters:
CONT. ARBITRATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-06-2006 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Donald L. Jageman, Esq., appearing specially appearing for Defendant. The court having reviewed the file finds no good cause or substancial justification for plaintiff's failure to comply with the court's orders and rules. Therefore, It is hereby ordered that this matter is dismissed. The court finds that a less severe sanction would not be effective due to the history of lack of compliance. Proceedings reported by: Gena Eales, CSR 176. (212)

Ntc Of Mo To Compel Depositions Of Pltfs
Off Calendar May-08-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant Maria Gutierrez's motion to compel depositions of plaintiffs is granted. No opposition has been filed. Plaintiffs are ordered to contact counsel for defendant to schedule their depositions. The depositions are to be completed within 21 days of notice of this order. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $340 are to be paid within 21 days of notice of this order. Prevailing party to submit form of order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Defendant Maria Gutierrez'S Motion To Compel Depositions Of Plaintiffs (Set In Dept. 612 In Error, Time Reserved For Odd Number 438453)
Off Calendar Mar-24-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant's motion to compel depositions of plaintiffs is ordered off calendar as it was not timely noticed. CCP Section 1005 requires that motions be served and filed 16 court days prior to the hearing, with five calendar days added if the motion was served by mail. The motion was filed on March 6, 2006. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Notice Of Motion To Compel Depositions Of Pltfs
Off Calendar Mar-21-2006 Text Ruling

CONT. ARBITRATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-21-2006 Continued To Jul-06-06 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212
MINI MINUTES: Michael J. Smith, Esq., appearing for Defendant. Plaintiff in Propria Persona is sanctioned $250.00, for failure to appear. The Arbitrator's jurisdiction is extended to 6/9/2006. The OSC is re-set to 7/6/2006 at 1:30 PM, in Department 212. Proceedings reported by: Carl Sauceda, CSR#2031. (212)
